# Implementation Plan: Sticky Navigation Bar

**Branch**: `004-sticky-nav-bar` | **Date**: February 1, 2026 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/004-sticky-nav-bar/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit.plan` command. See `.specify/templates/commands/plan.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

Create a persistent navigation bar component that remains fixed at the top of the viewport across all pages. The navigation bar features a two-section layout: left-aligned brand link ("grobsizziert.de") and right-aligned navigation links ("über", "alle posts"). The component uses CSS position:fixed with 50% transparency, maintains accessibility standards (Lighthouse Accessibility ≥95), and provides visual feedback for current page and hover states. A basic placeholder about page will be created to ensure all navigation links are functional.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5.x  
**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js 16.1.4, React 19.2.3, Tailwind CSS 4.x  
**Storage**: Static markdown files (existing blog posts + new about page)  
**Testing**: Not required for this feature (per constitution - tests are optional)  
**Target Platform**: Web (static HTML/CSS/JS via Next.js build)  
**Project Type**: Next.js App Router static site (personal blog)  
**Performance Goals**: Lighthouse Performance ≥90, Accessibility ≥95, Core Web Vitals pass (LCP <2.5s, FID <100ms, CLS <0.1)  
**Constraints**: 
- No new runtime dependencies (CSS-only solution with Tailwind)
- Server Component for navigation (no client-side state needed)
- Zero layout shift requirement (CLS = 0)
- Horizontal layout maintained on all screen sizes
- Transparency must maintain readability

**Scale/Scope**: Small personal blog (~10-20 pages), single navigation component used site-wide

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

**Required Checks** (based on my-blog constitution v1.0.0):

- [x] **Minimal Dependencies**: No new dependencies required - using only Tailwind CSS (already in project) for styling and positioning
- [x] **Component-First**: Navigation bar will be a self-contained React component (`components/Navigation.tsx`) with TypeScript interfaces for props
- [⚠️] **Static-First**: ⚠️ VIOLATION: Navigation component requires Client Component for active page detection
- [x] **Type Safety**: All components will have explicit TypeScript interfaces. Navigation props interface, page metadata types
- [x] **Accessibility & Performance**: Design meets all requirements:
  - Semantic HTML (`<nav>`, `<a>` elements)
  - Keyboard navigation built-in (standard links)
  - CSS-only transparency (no JS = zero performance impact)
  - Fixed positioning with proper z-index (no layout shift)
  - Target: Lighthouse Performance ≥90, Accessibility ≥95 ✓

**Post-Research Update**: One violation identified (see Complexity Tracking below)

**Result**: ⚠️ One justified violation - proceeding with Client Component for pathname detection

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/[###-feature]/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit.plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit.plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit.tasks command - NOT created by /speckit.plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
app/
├── layout.tsx           # Update to include <Navigation /> component
├── page.tsx             # Home page (existing - lists all posts)
├── about/
│   └── page.tsx         # NEW: About page placeholder
└── [slug]/
    └── page.tsx         # Blog post detail pages (existing)

components/
├── Navigation.tsx       # NEW: Main navigation bar component
├── BlogCard.tsx         # Existing component (unchanged)
├── BlogPostContent.tsx  # Existing component (unchanged)
└── Header.tsx           # Existing component (may need evaluation for removal/integration)

lib/
├── types.ts             # Update with navigation types
├── posts.ts             # Existing (unchanged)
├── markdown.ts          # Existing (unchanged)
└── utils.ts             # Existing (unchanged)
```

**Structure Decision**: Using Next.js App Router structure (default for my-blog). Navigation component will be added to `components/Navigation.tsx` as a shared Server Component. The component will be imported and rendered in `app/layout.tsx` to appear on all pages. A new route `app/about/page.tsx` will be created for the about page. No tests directory needed (tests optional per constitution, not requested in spec).

## Complexity Tracking

> **Fill ONLY if Constitution Check has violations that must be justified**

| Violation | Why Needed | Simpler Alternative Rejected Because |
|-----------|------------|-------------------------------------|
| Client Component for Navigation | Active page indication requires runtime pathname detection using Next.js `usePathname()` hook (per clarification #2: show visual indication for current page) | Server Component with path prop would require passing pathname from every page → increases coupling, maintenance burden, and repetitive code. CSS-only solutions (`:target`) don't work for full route paths. Minimal bundle impact (~1-2KB). |

**Justification Approved**: Active state indication is a functional requirement from spec (FR-014). The violation is minimal (pathname hook only, no state management), performance impact negligible, and alternative approaches significantly increase complexity.


## Phase 0: Research & Analysis (✅ COMPLETE)

**Objective**: Resolve all NEEDS CLARIFICATION items and establish technical approach

**Deliverable**: [research.md](research.md)

**Key Findings**:
1. CSS `position: fixed` chosen for always-visible navigation
2. Tailwind `bg-white/50` for 50% transparency
3. Client Component required for active page detection (usePathname hook)
4. Flexbox layout with responsive text sizing for horizontal mobile layout
5. Next.js Link component for client-side routing
6. Semantic HTML and ARIA attributes for accessibility

**Technologies Finalized**:
- Next.js 16.1.4 App Router
- React 19.2.3 (Client Component for pathname detection)
- TypeScript 5.x strict mode
- Tailwind CSS 4.x (no new dependencies)

**Status**: ✅ All research questions answered. No remaining unknowns.

---

## Phase 1: Design & Contracts (✅ COMPLETE)

**Objective**: Define data structures, component interfaces, and integration points

**Deliverables**:
- ✅ [data-model.md](data-model.md) - Entity definitions and relationships
- ✅ [contracts/navigation.d.ts](contracts/navigation.d.ts) - TypeScript interfaces
- ✅ [quickstart.md](quickstart.md) - Developer implementation guide

**Key Design Decisions**:
1. **NavigationLink interface**: `{ href: string, label: string }`
2. **NavigationProps interface**: `{ className?: string }` (optional styling)
3. **Component Structure**: Single Navigation.tsx with hardcoded links
4. **Active State Logic**: `usePathname()` with conditional styling
5. **Layout Integration**: Rendered in root layout with `pt-16` offset

**Files to Create**:
- `components/Navigation.tsx` (new)
- `app/about/page.tsx` (new)

**Files to Modify**:
- `app/layout.tsx` (add Navigation component)
- `lib/types.ts` (add NavigationLink, NavigationProps interfaces)

**Status**: ✅ Design complete. Ready for implementation.
